title: ericameria pulchella var. elatior
intro: Ericameria pulchella var. elatior, also known as Island Rubber Rabbitbrush, is a shrub native to the Channel Islands of California in the United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and even exhibiting potential anticancer properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Ericameria pulchella var. elatior are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
